title: Gooey, Cheesy Middle-Eastern Lamb Tagine
description: A rich, creamy, and spicy Middle-Eastern dish featuring tender lamb, gooey cheese, and a hint of unwaxed lime. Perfect for a lazy Sunday.

ingredients.name: feta cheese
ingredients.notes: dairy-free alternative: use cashew or soy-based cheese

instructions: Season the lamb cubes with salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb cubes and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté the chopped onion and minced garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually pour in the almond milk, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and cook, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the almond yogurt, lime juice, and feta cheese (or dairy-free alternative).
Simmer the tagine, uncovered, for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the sauce has thickened.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs and a dollop of yogurt, if desired.
